The reference value for North Macedonia as of late October 2025 is 1.000, indicating an average of 0 'Explosions/Remote violence' events over the preceding 360 days. For the criteria to be met, North Macedonia would need to experience more than 10 times this baseline—meaning at least 11 politically motivated remote violence/explosion events within a single 30-day period. 

ACLED strictly codes political violence and conflict events. As of the November 9, 2025 knowledge cutoff, North Macedonia remains generally peaceful with no ongoing armed conflicts, terror campaigns, or insurgencies that would produce anywhere near 11 bombing or remote violence incidents in a given month. The last time the country saw substantial remote violence was during the 2001 insurgency. 

For the first resolution date (November 16, 2025), 23 of the 30 days have already passed with no publicly reported events, meaning 11 attacks would have to occur in just a 7-day window—a virtual impossibility. While uncertainty technically rises the further out we forecast (December 2025 and February 2026), the underlying baseline probability of a sudden, massive onset of armed conflict involving repeated explosives/remote violence in North Macedonia within the next few months remains essentially zero. I assign the baseline minimum probability allowed (0.05) to the nearest date, slightly increasing my uncertainty for the subsequent dates as instructed.

The Falkland Islands is a highly peaceful British Overseas Territory with no active armed conflicts, insurgencies, or violent organized gangs. For the question to resolve positively, the ACLED dataset would need to record at least 11 events strictly coded as "Battles" (violent interactions between organized armed groups) in the 30-day windows preceding the respective resolution dates. Based on a review of recent events up to the November 9, 2025 cutoff date, there are utterly no indications of armed conflict, political violence, or military escalation (e.g., from Argentina) occurring or brewing. Given that the baseline of battles is zero, jumping to 11+ battles within 30 days essentially requires a full-scale sudden war, the probability of which is functionally zero. In accordance with the minimum probability bounds and the instruction to increase uncertainty for more distant forecast horizons, the probabilities are set to the lowest permissible values, increasing slightly over time.

The spread recently experienced a rapid increase from around 1.05 at the end of October to 1.16 on November 7 (the latest available trading point before the November 9 cutoff). A value of 1.16 is relatively high for this series over the past month, acting as a local peak (with a max of 1.17 on Nov 4). Due to the likelihood of mean reversion after such an abrupt short-term jump, the probability that the spread will be strictly higher than this elevated threshold on future resolution dates is less than 50%. 

The FRED tool's statistical analysis provides an estimate of ~34% for an increase in the very short term (1 week). Given the volatility and mean-reverting nature of bond spreads, I am assigning a 36% probability for the 2025-11-16 resolution date. For the longer-term dates, prediction uncertainty inherently scales up, driving the probabilities closer to 0.5 (43% for 2025-12-09 and 47% for 2026-02-07), reflecting the base rate uncertainty of long-horizon market shifts but maintaining a slight lean against the currently elevated comparative baseline.

The US Corporate Bond effective yield has experienced a broader downtrend over the past 6–8 months, declining from approximately 5.38% in April 2025 to around 4.67% in late October 2025, before experiencing a minor bounce to 4.84% by November 7 (the likely anchor value for November 9's reference point). The official FRED tool statistical analysis projects a combined near-term probability of an increase at 0.49, balancing the recent bounce and seasonal factors against the prevailing downward trend. Because short-term momentum favors stabilization or a slight mean-reverting decline in yield along the recent trendline, I evaluate the near-term probability of an increase slightly below a coin toss (0.48). As instructed, uncertainty requires probabilities to be pulled closer to 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends, reflecting the unpredictability of Federal Reserve policy actions and broader macroeconomic shifts at the 1-month and 3-month marks. Consequently, the probabilities are monotonically moderated toward 0.5 over the subsequent dates.

The latest available data from FRED up to November 7, 2025 places the ICE BofA Corporate Index OAS at 0.84, a sharp, localized spike from recent levels (0.75 just a couple of weeks prior) but still historically tight. Since 0.84 acts as a local peak, the short-term expectation (Nov 16, just one week later) implies a likely pullback or settling following election-week volatility, making a further increase statistically less likely. The FRED statistical tool estimates a ~35% combined probability of exceeding this threshold by mid-November. Over the medium (Dec 9) to longer term (Feb 7), the historically compressed nature of these spreads (10-year average >1.20) and rising macro risks (as noted by IMF and M&G Investments) present significant asymmetric upside risk for the spreads to widen. Thus, the probability of exceeding the 0.84 baseline increases over time. Conforming to the guideline that uncertainty must increase over the forecast horizon (probabilities must converge closer to 0.5), I forecast 35%, 43%, and 51% respectively.

The FRED fetch gives a baseline P(increase) of 0.69 based on the strong likelihood of mean reversion after recent downward momentum, as the current value of 2891 remains significantly below the trendline estimate (~3043). The overall linear trend is negative (-1.774 per day), which implies that over time, the long-term decline (potentially from QT or liquidity shifts) may pull values back down below the 2025-11-09 threshold again. I apply this 0.68 estimate for the near-term horizon. For longer horizons, I smoothly push the probability closer to 0.5 representing increased uncertainty. The FRED data shows the TGA value as of Nov 5, 2025 was 942.7 billion. The latest Treasury presentation from Nov 5 assumes an end-of-year 2025 cash balance target of 850 billion, and an end-of-March 2026 target of 850 billion. Because the current level (942.7B) is considerably higher than the 850B target for both Q4 2025 and Q1 2026, the TGA is expected to gradually decrease as the Treasury manages issuance and outlays towards that goal. Consequently, the probability of the balance increasing above 942.7B is relatively low in the short term. The FRED statistical tool gives a 35% combined historical/trend probability of exceeding the threshold for the near term. We reflect this drop toward the target with ~0.30 for the nearest date, gradually increasing our uncertainty to 0.40 for the 3-month horizon (Feb 2026) due to typical cash flow volatility and the longer timeframe.

The FRED series for DEXUSUK shows a recent downward linear trend (slope = -0.00087/day), with the value dropping from ~1.35 in September to 1.317 by November 7, 2025. The statistical projection based on the linear model yields a prediction of ~1.307 for November 16, with a P(exceeds threshold) of 0.31. Despite a minor rebound in the last three days (from 1.304 to 1.317), the overall trend remains down over the past month. I will rely on the statistical estimate as my primary anchor for the near term, setting the first probability slightly above the model calculation to account for recent momentum. For the more distant horizons, uncertainty increases, and macroeconomic shifts or differing monetary policies between the Federal Reserve and Bank of England could reverse the trend, so I adjust the probabilities progressively closer to 0.5.

On November 9, 2025, Hikaru Nakamura's rating on the Wikipedia FIDE rankings page was 2813 (derived from the November 2025 FIDE list). For his rating to be "at least 1% higher", it would need to increase by over 28 Elo points, reaching ~2842. 

1. **November 16, 2025:** FIDE official ratings are updated monthly on the 1st. Therefore, the November 16 rating on Wikipedia will be identical to the November 9 rating, meaning a 0% change. 
2. **December 9, 2025 & February 7, 2026:** Gaining 28+ Elo points is an incredibly steep task for a player already rated >2800. It would require multiple dominant performances against fellow super-GMs or flawless streaks in lower-tier tournaments. Attaining 2842 would arguably make Nakamura the world's highest-rated player (surpassing Magnus Carlsen) and mark one of the highest peak ratings in chess history. Rather than risking his current world #2 standing, Nakamura has been selectively playing lower-tier open tournaments (like the Iowa Open and Maritime Open) solely to meet the 40-game requirement for the 2026 FIDE Candidates tournament. Sweeping these farm tournaments against 2200-2400 level players yields negligible Elo gains (e.g., 1-2 points) but carries huge downside risk if he draws even once. Therefore, a massive 28-point spike in his rating over the next 1-3 months carries virtually no realistic probability.

I assign the floor probability (0.05) to the near-term dates and very slightly higher probabilities for distant dates to reflect fundamental, structural uncertainty over a longer horizon.

Although the UK launched a world-first targeted gonorrhea vaccination program in August 2025 (using the Bexsero/4CMenB cross-protective vaccine), the resolution of this question depends exclusively on whether the "Vaccine(s)" column is updated for Gonorrhea on the Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases". 

A direct fetch of that Wikipedia page on the knowledge cutoff date (November 9, 2025) reveals that the table has not been edited since October 1, 2025. More notably, the "Vaccine(s)" column appears systematically unmaintained and is left completely blank for virtually all infectious diseases listed—even for those with globally ubiquitous vaccines such as Hepatitis A/B, Influenza, Cholera, and Diphtheria.

Because the entire column is neglected, the natural probability of a spontaneous, isolated update occurring specifically for Gonorrhea is extremely low. Even if a forecaster attempts to edit the page to resolve the question as "Yes", such an isolated edit to a totally blank column is highly conspicuous and liable to be reverted by Wikipedia administrators for consistency. As a result, the probability starts near the minimum allowed value for the shortest time horizon. 

Given the rule that uncertainty increases over time, the probabilities grow incrementally for the more distant dates, representing the cumulative but still slim chance that either Wikipedia editors undertake a massive project to populate the entire vaccine column, or the ongoing UK rollout generates enough sustained buzz to prompt a lasting anomalous edit.

Thus, the likelihood remains extremely slim, though uncertainty marginally increases as the time horizon extends to February 2026.

To determine if Shakhriyar Mamedyarov's Elo rating will be at least 1% higher than his 2025-11-09 rating, we calculate the required threshold. The November 2025 FIDE rating (and thus the Wikipedia page reading on 2025-11-09) lists his rating at 2742. A 1% increase requires him to reach 2769.42, which practically means a rating of 2770 or higher. 

For 2025-11-16: FIDE ratings are published officially on the 1st of each month. Wikipedia updates its "Top players" tables to reflect these monthly releases. The Wikipedia rankings on November 16 will continue to display the November 1 list (2742), meaning there is virtually zero chance of an increase by this first resolution date. I am predicting 0.05 as it is the minimum threshold allowed.

For 2025-12-09: This will capture the December 2025 FIDE rating list. In November, Mamedyarov is playing in the FIDE World Cup. Because his rating is relatively high (2742) and he is 40 years old, his K-factor is 10. Gaining 28 classical Elo points in a single month requires an extraordinary performance (an excess score of +2.8 above expectation against top super-GMs). While theoretically possible if he crushes his opposition en route to the World Cup Finals, such sudden rating surges are exceedingly rare. 

For 2026-02-07: By the release of the February 2026 rating list, Mamedyarov will have had three months to push his rating up. Though this longer timeframe allows for more tournament action (such as Wijk aan Zee or other events), older veterans generally maintain or slowly lose rating points; climbing back into the 2770 echelon (around world top 10) is fundamentally improbable. Uncertainty modestly increases given the expanded time horizon.

The disease "Pasteurellosis" has long-existing veterinary vaccines (like the fowl cholera vaccine developed by Louis Pasteur), but no human vaccine exists, as human infections are typically rare (arising mostly from animal bites) and are easily treated with antibiotics like penicillin. There are no ongoing clinical trials or active developments for a human Pasteurellosis vaccine reported in recent literature.

The resolution strictly depends on the "List of infectious diseases" Wikipedia page. The Wikipedia API confirms that as of the forecast date (2025-11-09), the latest revision to that page was on 2025-10-01. This means the page is relatively inactive, and no secret updates occurred between late October and the November 9 cutoff. Given that the page has remained historically stable with an empty/‘No’ status for the "Vaccine(s)" column for Pasteurellosis, the likelihood of a spontaneous edit adding animal vaccines is very low. 

Because the article heavily skews toward human medicine, adding a purely veterinary vaccine might even be reverted by editors. Consequently, the only pathways to a "Yes" resolution are a completely unexpected breakthrough in human vaccine development (highly improbable) or a random/market-driven Wikipedia edit that survives moderation. Therefore, the probabilities for an update are kept near the absolute minimum for the near term, gradually widening towards 0.5 for the longer forecast horizons to account for background uncertainty and random wiki edits.

By checking the "FIDE rankings" Wikipedia page as it existed just before the cutoff date, we determined that Wei Yi was listed with an Elo rating of 2753 on the 1 November 2025 ranking list.
A 1% increase over 2753 would require an Elo rating of at least 2780.53, making 2781 the absolute minimum target to achieve a YES resolution.

1. **2025-11-16:** FIDE publishes rating lists only once per month, and the Wikipedia page updates to reflect these official monthly lists. On November 16, the page will exactly mirror the November 1 rating of 2753. Because 2753 is not mathematically greater than itself by 1%, the true probability here is strictly 0. However, constrained by the 0.05 minimum floor, I estimate this at 0.05.

2. **2025-12-09:** This resolution date will reflect the December 1, 2025 FIDE ratings. At the forecast cutoff, Wei Yi is participating in the 2025 FIDE World Cup. Gaining ~28 classical rating points in a single FIDE tournament for a 2750+ rated super-grandmaster is incredibly rare and statistically nearly impossible; it requires a massive positive score (+6 or +7 classical wins) against high-level opposition without losing. Furthermore, typical World Cup runs often involve draws in classical games followed by tiebreaks (which do not affect classical ratings). The chance of hitting 2781 in one month is minimal. Scaling for increased horizon uncertainty, I forecast 0.06.

3. **2026-02-07:** This date uses the February 1, 2026 FIDE list. Wei Yi's historical career peak FIDE rating was roughly 2755. Achieving 2781 would demand a historic, continuous run of dominance over several months, including potential events like the January 2026 Tata Steel Chess Tournament. While a longer timeframe gives him a mathematically better chance to accumulate points, jumping ~30 points above a career plateau at the absolute pinnacle of world chess is still extremely improbable. Increasing uncertainty for the longer time horizon, I forecast 0.08.

Enterobiasis (pinworm infection) is a mild, non-lethal parasitic infection that is easily and cheaply treated using anthelmintic medications such as mebendazole, albendazole, and pyrantel pamoate. There is neither a significant medical need nor an economic incentive to develop a vaccine against pinworms, and a web search reveals no evidence of any ongoing vaccine trials or development for Enterobius vermicularis. Thus, the likelihood of a vaccine being developed, approved, and subsequently listed on the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page within the next few months (by February 2026) is extremely low. I assign the minimum allowable probabilities, slightly increasing over time as per the instructions to reflect the expanding forecast horizon.

Moderna's Phase 3 trial for its norovirus vaccine candidate has been delayed and is now enrolling for case accrual over the 2025-2026 Northern Hemisphere winter season. Efficacy data from this trial will not be available until spring 2026 at the earliest, with regulatory submissions to follow. Other candidates (like Vaxart's) are in earlier stages, expecting to start Phase 3 in 2026. As it takes months to years to complete Phase 3 trials and secure regulatory approval, it is virtually impossible for a norovirus vaccine to be fully developed, licensed, and then reflected as such on the specified Wikipedia page by early 2026. The probabilities for the Wikipedia column changing to "Yes" across these short-to-medium-term dates are set near the minimum allowed, increasing slightly over time to account for the remote possibilities of a premature Wikipedia edit or an emergency authorization in another jurisdiction.
